questions.question: Which ancient text is considered the earliest known source of Indian philosophy?
questions.explanation: The Vedas are the earliest sources of Indian philosophy, dating back to around 1500 BCE or earlier.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Mughal Empire in India?
questions.explanation: Babur founded the Mughal Empire in India after his victory at the Battle of Panipat in 1526.

questions.question: Which act marked the beginning of the Civil Disobedience Movement in India's freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: The Salt Satyagraha in 1930 was a pivotal part of the Civil Disobedience Movement against colonial salt laws.

questions.question: Who was the leader of the Non-Cooperation Movement launched in 1920?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi spearheaded the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1920 as a mass protest against British rule.

questions.question: Which event directly led to the Partition of India in 1947?
questions.explanation: The Partition Plan of 1947 was the formal agreement that led to the partition of India.

questions.question: Which river is known as the 'Sorrow of Bihar'?
questions.explanation: The Koshi River is called the 'Sorrow of Bihar' due to frequent devastating floods.

questions.question: Which of the following is the largest saltwater lake in India?
questions.explanation: The Rann of Kutch is the largest salt desert and contains saline water bodies, though the question refers to saltwater lakes; Chilika Lake (not saltwater) is larger in India, so the closest correct answer here is Rann of Kutch for salt flats.

questions.question: Which climate zone is predominantly found in the Thar Desert?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert falls under the arid desert climate zone characterized by low rainfall.

questions.question: The 'Silver Line' project is associated with which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Silver Line project is a high-speed rail corridor in Kerala.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Justice of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India appoints the Chief Justice of India under constitutional provisions.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Articles 12 to 35 of the Constitution define Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: Who is the current Speaker of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: Om Birla has been serving as the Speaker of the Lok Sabha since 2019.

questions.question: The 'Judiciary' in India is primarily responsible for:
questions.explanation: The judiciary interprets and applies the law, ensuring justice.

questions.question: Which body is responsible for amending the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Amendments to the Constitution are made by the Parliament according to Article 368.

questions.question: The Union Budget of India is presented annually by:
questions.explanation: The Finance Minister presents the Union Budget in Parliament every year.

questions.question: India’s GDP is mainly contributed by which sector?
questions.explanation: The Service sector contributes the largest share to India's GDP.

questions.question: The 'Five Year Plans' in India were primarily formulated by:
questions.explanation: The Planning Commission was responsible for formulating Five Year Plans until 2017.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the Goods and Services Tax (GST)?
questions.explanation: GST aims to unify indirect taxation and simplify the tax structure.

questions.question: Which particle is responsible for carrying charge in an atom?
questions.explanation: Protons carry a positive charge in an atom.

questions.question: The process of photosynthesis primarily occurs in which part of the plant?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis primarily occurs in the leaves of plants.

questions.question: Which element is most abundant in the Earth's crust?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is the most abundant element in the Earth's crust.

questions.question: Which technology is used in the Global Positioning System (GPS)?
questions.explanation: GPS uses satellite navigation technology to determine location.

questions.question: Which animal is known as the 'King of the Jungle'?
questions.explanation: The lion is known as the 'King of the Jungle'.

questions.question: Which of the following is a renewable resource?
questions.explanation: Solar energy is a renewable resource.

questions.question: The Convention on Biological Diversity was adopted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Convention on Biological Diversity was adopted in 1992.

questions.question: Which protected area is known as the 'Eagle’s Nest' in India?
questions.explanation: Jim Corbett National Park is popularly known as the 'Eagle’s Nest'.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu has been serving as the President of India since 25 July 2022.

questions.question: Who is the current Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi has been the Prime Minister since 2014.

questions.question: Which country hosted the G20 summit in 2023?
questions.explanation: India hosted the G20 summit in 2023.

questions.question: Mohan Charan Majhi holds the position of Chief Minister of which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Mohan Charan Majhi is the current Chief Minister of Odisha since 12 June 2024.
